title: Multi-Agent Tradeoffs
concept: Modular design, higher precision, and clean context; but high token cost, latency, and deadlock risk.

exercises.prompt: What is a disadvantage of single-agent design?
exercises.explain: Single-agent designs can be harder to prompt-engineer compared to multi-agent designs.

cards.title: Understanding Tradeoffs
cards.body: When using multiple agents, design choices can offer benefits and drawbacks that can impact performance.

cards.title: Multi-Agent Design
cards.body: Multi-agent design involves using several agents that can work together to perform tasks. This design allows for more specialized and precise responses.

cards.title: Modular Design,
cards.body: Think of multi-agent design like a toolbox with different tools. Each tool (agent) is specialized for a specific job, allowing for greater efficiency.

cards.title: Higher Precision
cards.body: In a multi-agent system, one agent might handle language understanding while another focuses on generating responses, leading to more accurate results.

cards.title: Myth: Multi-Agent Systems Are Always Better
cards.body: Many believe multi-agent systems are superior, but they can have higher costs and complexity that may not be justifiable for certain tasks.

cards.title: Comparing Systems
cards.body: Let's compare single and multi-agent designs. Single agents are like a solo artist, while multi-agent designs resemble a full orchestra, each player taking their part.

exercises.prompt: Which is a tradeoff of multi-agent systems?
exercises.explain: Higher precision is a tradeoff of multi-agent systems due to their modular design.

exercises.prompt: Multi-agent systems can lead to deadlock risks. True or False?
exercises.explain: True. Multi-agent systems can experience deadlock when agents are waiting on each other to proceed.

exercises.prompt: Multi-agent systems have higher token cost, which means they can be more __________.
exercises.sentence: Multi-agent systems have higher token cost, which means they can be more expensive.
exercises.explain: The correct word 'expensive' describes the higher token cost associated with multi-agent systems.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about multi-agent systems is generated by AI?

options.text: They always work better than single-agent systems.
More agents can increase complexity and costs.
They don't have any risks associated with them.

exercises.explain: The AI-generated statement is correct because it accurately reflects the tradeoffs of multi-agent systems.

exercises.prompt: What are the disadvantages of multi-agent systems compared to single-agent designs?
exercises.explain: The correct answer highlights that multi-agent systems tend to be costlier and can face deadlock risks, which are significant disadvantages compared to single-agent systems.
